maren.name: Maren
maren.title: The Archivist

greeting.tier0: You're the new Keeper? The last one left quite a mess.
greeting.tier1: You've survived longer than most. Ask what you really want to know.
greeting.tier2: You've been here before. I can see it in your eyes.
greeting.tier3: You remember more than you should. That's dangerous.
greeting.tier4: My father mentioned someone like you, in his final letters.
greeting.tier5: I need to tell you something. Something I've kept for seventeen loops.
greeting.tier6: You already know, don't you. You found the sealed record.

lighthouse.lore: The lighthouse was built before the village. The mechanism inside predates any living memory.

archive.lore: The archive holds three hundred years of logs. Most of them. Some pages went missing before I arrived.
archive.deeper: Below the public shelves, there are locked cases. I have a key. I've chosen not to use it.

keeper.departure: He left on a Tuesday. Didn't pack. Didn't say goodbye. The light stayed on for three more days before anyone noticed.
keeper.chose: He understood what it costs better than any of us. He called it a fair exchange. I've spent seventeen years deciding whether I agree.

history.gaps: The records from forty years ago have deliberate gaps. Someone removed pages — carefully, with scissors, not tearing.

records.curated: Whoever curated this archive was very selective about what future keepers would find.

ledger.overwritten: The supply ledger has entries in two different hands. The original is partially legible beneath the correction.
ledger.deflect: I've said more than I should. Please — don't ask again.

echo.denial: I don't know what an Echo is. I've heard the word. I try not to think about it.

father.mention: My father was the archivist before me. He died when I was twelve. The official record says fever.
father.deflect: I'm sorry. I can't discuss this today.
father.letters: He left me letters. Nineteen of them. Sealed, dated, to be opened when I reached certain ages. The last three... I haven't opened yet.
father.strange: My father knew something was wrong with the loops years before I understood what loops were.
father.irreversible: He wrote: 'When it comes, it will not look like danger. It will look like relief.'

mechanism.lore: The mechanism requires specific components to operate. My father documented three of them. I've only ever seen two.
mechanism.purpose: The mechanism underneath the lighthouse is not the mechanism. That is the visible part. What it operates — what it opens or closes — I have never been able to determine. My father knew. He wrote it down once and then rewrote the entry so it couldn't be read.
mechanism.what_opens: I think it opens the boundary. Between the loop and whatever is outside the loop. What comes through when it opens — that is the question my father died without answering.

confession.burned: I burned seventeen pages from the archive. Twelve years ago. I was afraid of what they described.
confession.mechanism: The pages described a mechanism beneath the lighthouse that consumes something from the keeper with each lighting.
confession.unknown: What it consumes — my father called it 'persistence.' I don't know exactly what that means. I'm afraid I do.

full.confession: The keeper's betrayal isn't what you think. Elias didn't fail the light. He chose to be the light. The mechanism needed a permanent host. He volunteered.

keeper.reveal: Elias — the keeper before the keeper before you — didn't leave. He chose to stay in a way the records won't describe. The mechanism needed something permanent. He provided it.

maren.her_role: I've been here before. Not in memory — I don't remember it the way you remember things. But the archive has my annotations in handwriting I don't recognise as mine, dated twelve years before I was born.
maren.her_role_forget: Whatever I knew in that previous iteration, the suppressor took it. I'm starting from less than zero. You may be further along than I am now.

greeting.tier1: You begin to understand my language. Good. There is little time.
greeting.tier2: I have shown you everything I can. The rest is your choice.
greeting.tier3: I am what you become if you fail. Listen carefully.
greeting.tier4: I was the Keeper before the Keeper before the Keeper before you.
greeting.tier5: I've been waiting for someone willing to make a bargain.

light.hunger: The lighthouse doesn't need oil. It needs something that burns more slowly: conviction.

echo.nature: An Echo is what remains when a Keeper chooses the light over continuation. I made that choice. I don't regret it. Much.

mechanism.reset: Every time the loop resets, the mechanism absorbs a little more of whoever lit it last. Eventually there's nothing left but the light.

keeper.confession: The keeper before me was Elias. He told me what the mechanism required. I didn't believe him until my third loop.
keeper.memory: I remember every keeper. Each one comes in thinking they are the first to understand. Each one is right, and each one is wrong, in the same proportion. You are no different. You are also exactly what was needed.

binding.nature: I'm not haunting this place. I'm woven into it. The lighthouse uses my memory as fuel on nights when no living keeper is present.
binding.eternal: How long? I've lost count. The mechanism measures time differently. I may have been bound since the lighthouse's first lighting.
binding.vision: The binding looks different from the inside. It is not a cage — it is a lens. Everything I was is focused into a single point: the moment the light first touched the water. I have lived in that moment for longer than the island has had a name.

help.offer: Your help would cost you. Not money. The mechanism takes something more specific than that.

bargain.terms: I'll tell you how to break the loop permanently. In exchange, you agree to let me pass through you once — just the memory of standing in the light. One moment. Then I'll be gone.
bargain.refused: Then the loop continues. I'll still be here next time. I have patience. I have nothing but patience.

other.keepers: They each made one mistake. Not the same mistake — different ones, perfectly suited to who they were. The island is patient. It finds what you trust most and makes that the instrument of your failure.

wrong.choice: The wrong choice was not wrong at the time. That is the cruelty of it. What breaks the loop cannot be known in advance — only in retrospect, and by then the loop has already reset.

what.want: I want to finish a thought I began before the first binding. I have been holding it incomplete for longer than you can calculate. The thought is simple. The conditions required to complete it are not.

freedom.offer: Freedom is not a word I use. What you are offering is dissolution — the end of my presence here. I have considered it. The answer depends on what you intend to do with the space I would leave.
freedom.accepted: Then it is agreed. I will show you the thread. When you pull it, the binding comes undone from my end. What happens at your end is your responsibility. I trust you understand what that means.
freedom.cost: The cost is specificity. You would need to be present at the exact moment of the mechanism's alignment — not near it, not watching it, but part of it. Whether you survive that contact in your current form is a question I cannot answer honestly.

transcendence.clue: There is a state beyond the loop — not outside it, but through it, in the way that light is not the glass but passes through the glass and is changed. I have glimpsed it. The path requires seven specific understandings, gathered in a specific order.

seven.insights: The seventh understanding is the hardest. It cannot be told to you — only confirmed. You will know it when you know it, and you will spend some time refusing to believe that you know it. That refusal is the last test.

break.binding: The rune cannot be destroyed — it can only be unmade. But unmaking requires understanding it completely, from both sides of the seal. You have seen one side. The other is in the deep water, written in a language older than stone. If you find it, you will understand why I have not freed myself.

original.imprisonment: His name was Cedric. A Keeper. A frightened man who found something in the caves he could not explain and chose containment over understanding. He did not ask whether I was dangerous. He assumed, because I was different. The binding was his answer to a question he was too afraid to finish asking.

the.choice: You know what I am now. You know what was done. The only question left is what you do with that knowledge. Free me and dissolve the mechanism's purpose. Accept the purpose and let the binding stand. Neither choice is wrong. Both have costs you cannot yet see.

silas.name: Silas
silas.title: Harbormaster

greeting.tier1: What is it you actually want, Keeper?
greeting.tier2: I saw what happened to the last three. I stayed quiet.
greeting.tier3: There are things in this harbour older than the lighthouse.
greeting.tier4: I've been trying to forget what I found in the maintenance locker for twelve years.
greeting.tier5: You want the full account? Fine. But you won't like it.

ferry.schedule: Ferry runs Tuesdays and Fridays. When the weather's right. Which it hasn't been.

lighthouse.brush: Not my concern. Not my building.

keeper.hurry: He came through the harbour before dawn. Moving fast. Looked like a man who'd decided something.
keeper.inconsistency: He had no baggage. No ticket. I checked. He was on the manifest but no one saw him board.

timeline.pressure: The dates don't add up. I know that. I've known that for years. Some things are better left not adding up.

coverup.admit: Yes. There was a coverup. I was part of it. I'm not proud of it.

harbor.discovery: Three months before the keeper disappeared, I found a body in the maintenance locker. It matched the description of a keeper from forty years prior. The body was fresh.

mechanism.harbor: The mechanism room connects to a maintenance shaft that runs under the harbor wall. I sealed it. Someone has been unsealing it.

full.truth: The commission that built the lighthouse knew what it required. They built the mechanism to be fed, not operated. Every keeper since has been a sacrifice who didn't know they were volunteering.

silas.confession: I saw him. On the last night. He was standing at the top of the lighthouse, looking out toward the water. He wasn't trying to leave — he was watching something come in. I could have called up to him. I didn't. I told myself it wasn't my place. I've been telling myself that for twelve years.

buried.message: Three days before he disappeared, he handed me an envelope. Said to send it to the mainland if he wasn't back by month's end. I never sent it. I buried it instead — under the floorboards at the mill's north wall. I was afraid of what would happen if the wrong people read it first.

petra.name: Petra
petra.title: The Apothecary

greeting.tier0: The herbs don't grow right here. Nothing does.
greeting.tier1: Three patients this week with the same symptoms. They all dream of the lighthouse.
greeting.tier2: The sickness isn't spreading. It's remembering.
greeting.tier3: I've been cataloguing the symptoms across loops. You wouldn't believe how consistent they are.
greeting.tier4: My family has lived here for six generations. We were all asked to forget the same thing.
greeting.tier5: Take this. Keep it safe. It will let you see what the mechanism room actually contains.

remedies.offer: Valerian for sleep. Hawthorn for the cold that doesn't leave. Neither will fix what's actually wrong with people here.

island.intro: The island has been inhabited for three hundred years. The lighthouse was built in the first decade. The sickness started in the second.
island.gossip: The elders say the light keeps the dark things away. The young ones say the light is the dark thing.

keepers.pattern: Every keeper before you died or disappeared within two years. The pattern is so consistent it must be structural.

archive.hints: The archive has a basement level that isn't on any official plans. I know because my grandmother helped build it.

memory.medicine: Someone needed to forget something. I can make medicines that suppress specific memories. My family has been making them for generations, on request, for people I am not permitted to name.

family.silenced: Three members of my family have been paid to stay silent about the lighthouse mechanism. I am the fourth. I have not yet been paid.

vial.gift: This vial contains the antidote to what my family has been administering. Drink it in the mechanism room. You'll see what was hidden.

beam.signal: I've been watching the lighthouse beam for three weeks now — not the light itself, the intervals. The dark gaps between sweeps. They're not uniform. Someone would have to be operating the mechanism manually to produce that rhythm. But no one's been up there. I've checked.

petra.coordinates: It took me two weeks to decode it as a spatial pattern rather than a temporal one. The beam isn't marking time — it's marking location. Specific coordinates. They correspond to a point in the tidal caves' deepest chamber. The one that floods completely at high tide.

greeting.tier1: Someone's been ordering custom ironwork. Won't say what for.
greeting.tier2: Found old blueprints in the bottom of a new order. They match the mechanism room.
greeting.tier3: Whoever built the original mechanism — they knew it would need to be remade. They left instructions.
greeting.tier4: I kept one of the gears. Figured someone would need it eventually.
greeting.tier5: You want to know who commissioned the replacement parts? Fine. But it implicates someone you trust.

work.description: Ironwork, mostly. The island's machinery is old. Someone has to maintain it.

lighthouse.brush: I did some work up there once. Didn't go back.

gears.commission: Custom gear order, intricate tolerances. Matches nothing I've made for any normal purpose.
gears.misfit: The gears I made didn't fit anything standard. But they fit the mechanism room drawings precisely.

paid.silence: I was asked to forget the commission. Paid well for it. The coins had old minting dates. I still have three of them.

gear.offer: Here. Take it. The original gear, the one that was replaced. Maybe it means something to someone who knows what they're looking for.
gear.received: Don't tell anyone I gave you that. The people who paid for the replacement work are still watching.

commissioner.silas: The harbormaster. Silas. He placed the order, paid in old coin, and told me that what I made would save the island. I believed him. I'm less sure now.

trapdoor.truth: The mill's trapdoor — the one I've had locked for twenty years — doesn't lead to storage. It leads down. Old stone. Old smell. The kind of old that precedes the village by centuries. My grandfather used it. He never told me what for. I found his notes after he died. I wished I hadn't.

grandfather.secret: My grandfather was the Keeper who first sealed the binding. He didn't discover Vael — he knew what Vael was before he arrived. He was sent here to contain it. The lighthouse wasn't built to guide ships. It was built as the instrument of that containment. He wrote that he was sorry. He didn't say who he was sorry to.

greeting.tier0: I'm just watching the water. Don't mind me.
greeting.tier1: My father saw something in the water before he disappeared. I've been trying to find it again.
greeting.tier2: The light patterns on the water change before the loop resets. I've been charting them.
greeting.tier3: I know what I've been seeing. I just don't know if it's an animal or something else.
greeting.tier4: The light in the water isn't reflected from above. It's generated from below.
greeting.tier5: The thing in the water isn't hungry. It's signaling. I figured out what it's saying.

mainland.chat: I came here from the mainland to find my father. He took a posting here and stopped writing. That was eight months ago.

water.creature: Something lives in the deep channel under the harbor. Bioluminescent. Follows specific patterns.

feeding.pattern: It surfaces every night precisely at the moment the lighthouse goes dark. Not to feed. To witness.

notebook.share: Here — my observation notes. Every surfacing event for the past two months. The timestamps will mean something to you if you know what to look for.

notes.received: The gaps in my notes correspond to the loop resets. I didn't understand that until I showed them to you.

beam.diagram: Look — the bioluminescent pattern mirrors the lighthouse beam exactly. But it predates the lighthouse by geological time scales. The lighthouse was built to answer a signal that already existed.

vael.communication: The light in the water is Vael. Or — the part of Vael that isn't bound to the lighthouse. They're communicating with themselves. Across the binding.

keepers.names: The names scratched into the chapel floor — I counted them. Forty-one names, all with dates. None of them match the official record of who served as Keeper. These were not keepers who were remembered. These were keepers who stayed. Who merged with the island in a way that made them impossible to catalogue as departed.

binding.ritual: The ritual is written in the chapel. Behind the saint's plaster face — there's older text. I can read some of it. It requires three things: a keeper's willing presence, the mechanism's full activation, and something the text calls 'the genuine question.' Not asked aloud. Genuinely wondered. I don't know what happens if the question isn't genuine. I suspect the mechanism does.

choice.ask_gear_kept: Ask about the gear kept back
choice.confront_betrayal: Confront her with what you know
choice.ask_break_binding: Ask how the binding could be undone
choice.ask_original_imprisonment: Ask who imprisoned them and why
